title: hieracium cirritum subsp. ustulatum
intro: Hieracium cirritum subsp. ustulatum, also known as Ustulate Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems contain flavonoids, saponins, and other compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, Ustulate Hawkweed is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
